note: The pitch kit — templates the desk fills and fires. Tokens: {CLIENT} {PRICE} {MONTHLY} {LINK}. Rendered in M Pay's You tab; every template is a door the network already has open.

templates.name: The Rebuild
templates.tagline: Small business website — same spend, whole new machine
templates.body:
{CLIENT} — here's the whole plan in four sentences.

Right now part of your monthly spend buys hosting for a site that doesn't sell. I rebuild it modern and fast, move it to hosting that costs $0/month, and wire in the tools your business actually needs — so the same money you're already spending starts buying marketing instead of servers.

The numbers: {PRICE} once to build and cut over. {MONTHLY}/month after that — and that's a service retainer (content, edits, the marketing calendar), not a software bill.

The demo is already live — open it and see your business in it: {LINK}

templates.name: The Bookstore Special
templates.tagline: Sell every book ever printed without stocking one more shelf
templates.body:
{CLIENT} — you told me you wanted the search bar without the other website. Done — it's on YOUR site now.

The new build searches millions of titles right on your page — in print, out of print, rare — and every order routes through your store. Ingram fulfills in 1-3 days; the customer picks it up with a coffee. You just sold a book you never had to shelve.

And the plan you were considering at $50/month? It was retired in 2024 — the replacement runs ~$175/month plus dues. Mine runs $0 in software.

{PRICE} once to switch it on. {MONTHLY}/month for the content, the marketing calendar, and me. Demo's live: {LINK}

templates.name: The Space
templates.tagline: Your booking system, invoices, promotion, and messages — one app
templates.body:
{CLIENT} — one link replaces four tools.

Your space gets a booking page (calendar, time windows, your availability rules — like a Calendly you own), payment-request links you can text to anybody (like a cash app, but the record is a signed deal), self-serve promotion on the network floor, and a message thread inside every booking so nothing lives in DMs.

Google bookings still find you — but now they land in YOUR system, not a third party's.

{PRICE} to onboard the room, {MONTHLY}/month to run it. Your page would be live this week: {LINK}

templates.name: The Limited Offer
templates.tagline: The weekly content system — three seats, ever

templates.name: The Artist Offer
templates.tagline: $1,000 for a feature — and the thousand comes with a partner
